summary: Matched a large set of Kirby special-move functions, primarily in ftKb_SpecialN.c, including grounded/air SpecialN anim/capture/spit/drink paths, SpecialAirLw_IASA, CaSpecialAirN_IASA, and partial work on fn_800F53AC. The PR also cleaned up the Kirby item distance helper it_802F23AC by replacing raw int*/float* pointer arithmetic with typed Item_GObj*/Vec3 access. The decomp.dev bot reported +3608 matched bytes, 16 new function matches, and one unmatched function improved to 98.51%.
what_changed: Implemented or matched multiple previously stubbed Kirby SpecialN-related animation functions: ftKb_SpecialN_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirN_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNCapture0_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNCapture_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNSpit_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirNSpit_Anim, and ftKb_SpecialNDrink_Anim.

Matched ftKb_SpecialAirLw_IASA by factoring the stone-release countdown/input test into fbKb_SpecialLw_IASA_Inline and preserving x221C_b4 across the transition to ftKb_MS_SpecialAirLwEnd before spawning effect 0x49C.

Added fn_800F53AC implementation for Kirby hat/item spawning and cleanup: it_802ADC54 creates the item, x1984_heldItemSpec is updated, death/take-damage callbacks are installed, effect 0x496 or 0x497 is spawned depending on ground/air state, and cmd_vars[0] == 2 cleans up via it_802ADC34.

Added SpecialN helper inlines for shared state setup: ftKb_SpecialN_SetHitlagCb sets motion state plus effect hitlag pause/resume callbacks, ftKb_SpecialN_SetThrowCb configures capture/release/throw callbacks through ftCommon_8007E2D0, ftKb_SpecialNCapture_EnterState enters capture states and clears throw state with ftCommon_8007E2F4.

Improved inhaled-item/fighter distance checks in ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8, ftKb_SpecialNCapture0_Anim, and ftKb_SpecialNCapture_Anim by using Vec3 initialized from fp->cur_pos and comparing against the squared inhale velocity.

Matched ftKb_CaSpecialAirN_IASA, computing Copy Ability projectile launch velocity from left-stick Y, specialn_ca axis range attributes, angle difference, sinf/cosf, forward momentum, and facing direction.
Improved fn_800F98F4 by using GET_FIGHTER, ftParts_GetBoneIndex(FtPart_LHandN), spawning Kirby Luigi fire with it_802C01AC, and spawning effect 0x4B1 on the hand joint.
Added includes needed by the new code, including ef/efasync.h, ft/ftcoll.h, it/itCommonItems.h, it/items/itdrmariopill.h, and it/items/itmariofireball.h.

Changed it_802F23AC signature from double it_802F23AC(int* obj, float* pos) to f64 it_802F23AC(Item_GObj* obj, Vec3* pos), and rewrote the implementation to use GET_ITEM(gobj) and SQ(ip->pos.* - pos->*) instead of raw offset comments and casts.
smart_moves: Factored repeated SpecialN state setup into small static inline helpers, which allowed ground and air versions to share logic while still giving the compiler inline shapes suitable for matching.

Kept evidence of matching-sensitive constructs explicit with PAD_STACK, fake self-assignment, comma-operator/regalloc comments, and an empty fake-match branch from the permuter instead of hiding them as ordinary gameplay logic.
Moved ftKirbyDmgInline and ftKirbyDmgInline2 near the top as static inline helpers and removed unnecessary callback casts, relying on proper callback types already available in the fighter struct.
Used squared-distance comparisons against SQ or velocity squared, avoiding a sqrt and matching the helper it_802F23AC behavior.
Typed the item-distance API with Item_GObj and Vec3, which made callsites cleaner and eliminated unsafe int*/float* casts in Kirby inhale code.
Split fn_800F53AC into SpawnEffect and CleanupItem inline helpers, isolating effect/hitlag-callback setup and item cleanup while leaving the command-variable state machine readable.

Preserved exact side effects around capture/spit/drink transitions: ftCommon_8007E2F4 cleanup, ftCo_800DE2CC linkage, victim callbacks such as ftCo_800BDB58 or ftCo_800BE000, and resetting cmd_vars[0].
decomp_lessons: For paired ground/air fighter states, write one inline helper for the shared callback/state setup and pass only the motion-state IDs or callback variants that differ; this can improve both readability and matching.
When matching code that uses position-distance thresholds, prefer typed Vec3 and SQ expressions if they preserve codegen; this can replace raw pointer arithmetic without losing the match.

Do not remove weird-looking padding or fake operations if the diff says they are required by the permuter or regalloc; PAD_STACK, self-assignment, and empty branches may be intentional matching artifacts.
Callback assignment order can matter. The PR kept both ftKirbyDmgInline and ftKirbyDmgInline2 because death2_cb/take_dmg_cb are assigned in different orders for different matching contexts.
Effect spawning in fighter animation callbacks often needs the hitlag pause/resume callbacks set in the same block, not just efAsync_Spawn or efSync_Spawn.
When implementing Kirby inhale/capture paths, distinguish item targets from fighter victims: item paths call it_802F23AC/it_802F2810, while fighter paths call ftCo_800BD19C/ftCo_800BD620.
Header fixes can unlock cleaner matching work: correcting it_802F23AC's prototype removed local casts in ftKb_SpecialN.c and made the item helper easier to reuse.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter functions retain the ftKb_ prefix, with state/action suffixes such as SpecialN, SpecialAirN, SpecialNCapture, SpecialNSpit, SpecialNDrink, and CaSpecialAirN.
Shared local matching helpers were named with the target function or behavior followed by _inline, such as ftKb_SpecialNSpit_Anim_inline and ftKb_SpecialNDrink_Anim_inline.
Helper names for decomposed unknown functions use the original symbol plus a descriptive suffix, for example fn_800F53AC_SpawnEffect, fn_800F53AC_CleanupItem, and fn_800F9260_DrMario.
Kirby data attributes are treated as ftKb_DatAttrs* rather than struct ftKb_DatAttrs*, matching the project typedef style.
The item helper keeps its address-style name it_802F23AC but its prototype now exposes semantic types Item_GObj* and Vec3*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK with sizes including 16, 20, 24, and 32 bytes to match stack layout in several newly matched functions.
Used fake-match artifacts explicitly: an empty if (!da->specialhi_projectile_spawn_x) block, fp->facing_dir = fp->facing_dir self-assignments, and comments noting permuter-derived fake matches.
Kept a comma-operator expression in ftKb_SpecialAirHi3_Phys for regalloc-sensitive code.
Adjusted variable declaration/initialization order, such as Vec3 pos = fp->cur_pos and temporary Fighter* aliases, to obtain matching register allocation.
Used static inline helpers rather than out-of-line functions for repeated code blocks so the compiler could produce duplicated code matching the original.
Removed a previous pragma dont_inline around ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 after rewriting the function shape with direct typed calls and padding.
Used boolean true/false in the shared SpecialLw IASA inline while keeping its control-flow structure intact.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 60.48% (+0.09%, +3608 bytes), with 16 new matches and fn_800F53AC improved from 0.00% to 98.51%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching fn_800F53AC from 98.51% to 100%.
Search for other item helpers still using raw int* or float* prototypes that can be typed as Item_GObj* and Vec3*.
Look for later cleanup of fake-match constructs in ftKb_SpecialN.c, especially the empty branch and self-assignment comments.
Compare this PR's SpecialN helper inlining pattern against other Kirby copy ability matching PRs.
